Research & Papers.title: LCM: Lossless Context Management
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04050v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce Lossless Context Management (LCM), a deterministic architecture for LLM memory that outperforms Claude Code on long-context tasks. When benchmarked using Opus 4.6, our LCM-augmented coding agent, Volt, achieves higher scores than Claude Code on the OOLONG long-context eval, including at every context length between 32K and 1M tokens.
  LCM may be considered both a vindication and extension of the recursive paradigm pioneered by Recurs

Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04100v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Off-policy temporal-difference (TD) learning with function approximation faces a structural tradeoff among stability, projection geometry, and variance control. Emphatic TD (ETD) improves the off-policy projection geometry through follow-on emphasis, but the follow-on trace can have high variance. We revisit this tradeoff through Bellman-error centering. Although centering naturally removes a common drift term from TD errors, we show that a naive

Abstract: Surgical team performance arises from complex interactions between technical execution and non-technical skills, including communication and coordination dynamics. However, current surgical AI systems predominantly model visual workflow signals, lacking structured representations of intraoperative team interactions over time. Research & Papers.title: ANDRE: An Attention-based Neuro-symbolic Differentiable Rule Extractor
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04193v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Inductive Logic Programming (ILP) aims to learn interpretable first-order rules from data, but existing symbolic and neuro-symbolic approaches struggle to scale to noisy and probabilistic settings. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04227v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Procedural tasks with multiple ordered steps are ubiquitous in daily life. Recent advances in multimodal large language models (MLLMs) have enabled personal assistants that support daily activities. However, existing systems primarily provide reactive guidance triggered by user queries, or limited proactive assistance for isolated short-term events rather than long-horizon procedural tasks. Research & Papers.title: Temporal Reasoning Is Not the Bottleneck: A Probabilistic Inconsistency Framework for Neuro-Symbolic QA
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04243v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Despite significant advances, large language models (LLMs) continue to exhibit brittle performance on complex temporal reasoning tasks. This failure mode is widely attributed to inherent deficits in autoregressive logical deduction. Research & Papers.title: Parallel Prefix Verification for Speculative Generation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04263v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We introduce PARSE (PArallel pRefix Speculative Engine), a speculative generation framework that accelerates large language model (LLM) inference by parallelizing prefix verification on a semantic level. Existing speculative decoding methods are fundamentally limited by token-level equivalence: the target model must verify each token, leading to short acceptance lengths and modest speedups. Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04312v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Static capabilities benchmarks suffer from saturation and contamination, making it difficult to track capabilities progress over time. We introduce Agent Island, a multiplayer simulation environment in which language-model agents compete in a game of interagent cooperation, conflict, and persuasion. Research & Papers.title: The Scaling Properties of Implicit Deductive Reasoning in Transformers
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04330v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: We investigate the scaling properties of implicit deductive reasoning over Horn clauses in depth-bounded Transformers. By systematically decorrelating provability from spurious features and enforcing algorithmic alignment, we find that in sufficiently deep models with a bidirectional prefix mask, implicit reasoning approaches explicit CoT performance across graph topologies and problem widths, though CoT remains necessary for depth extrapolation.

Research & Papers.title: When Context Hurts: The Crossover Effect of Knowledge Transfer on Multi-Agent Design Exploration
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04361v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: The prevailing assumption in agent orchestration is that more context is better. We test this on multi-agent software design across 10 tasks, 7 context-injection conditions, and over 2,700 runs, and find a crossover effect: the same artifact type improves design exploration on some tasks (up to 20$\times$ tradeoff coverage) and actively degrades it on others (up to 46% reduction). Research & Papers.title: Deployment-Relevant Alignment Cannot Be Inferred from Model-Level Evaluation Alone
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04454v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Alignment evaluation in machine learning has largely become evaluation of models. Influential benchmarks score model outputs under fixed inputs, such as truthfulness, instruction following, or pairwise preference, and these scores are often used to support claims about deployed alignment. Research & Papers.title: How Does Thinking Mode Change LLM Moral Judgments? A Controlled Instant-vs-Thinking Comparison Across Five Frontier Models
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: We evaluate whether enabling provider-exposed reasoning mode changes moral judgments within the same model checkpoint. Research & Papers.title: From Parameter Dynamics to Risk Scoring : Quantifying Sample-Level Safety Degradation in LLM Fine-tuning
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04572v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Safety alignment of Large Language Models (LLMs) is extremely fragile, as fine-tuning on a small number of benign samples can erase safety behaviors learned from millions of preference examples. Abstract: Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using Inertial Measurement Unit (IMU) sensors is a cornerstone of mobile health, smart environments, and human-computer interaction. Research & Papers.title: AuditRepairBench: A Paired-Execution Trace Corpus for Evaluator-Channel Ranking Instability in Agent Repair

Research & Papers.title: Budget-aware Auto Optimizer Configurator

Abstract: Optimizer states occupy massive GPU memory in large-scale model training. Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: General-purpose LLMs with web search are increasingly used to scout the competitive landscape of pharmaceutical pipelines. Abstract: Inductive Logic Programming (ILP) learns interpretable logical rules from data. Abstract: A Tree Markov Decision Problem (T-MDP) is a finite-horizon MDP with a starting state $s_{1}$, in which every state is reachable from $s_{1}$ through exactly one state-action trajectory. T-MDPs arise naturally as abstractions of decision making in sequential games with perfect recall, against stationary opponents. Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Large language model (LLM) multi-agent systems typically rely on rigid orchestration, committing either to flat per-query routing or to hand-engineered task decomposition, so decomposition depth, worker choice, and inference budget are not jointly optimized under one objective. Abstract: We evaluate an initial coding-agent system for ARC-AGI-3 in which the agent maintains an executable Python world model, verifies it against previous observations, refactors it toward simpler abstractions as a practical proxy for an MDL-like simplicity bias, and plans through the model before acting. Research & Papers.title: Learning Reconstructive Embeddings in Reproducing Kernel Hilbert Spaces via the Representer Theorem
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2601.05811v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Motivated by the growing interest in representation learning approaches that uncover the latent structure of high-dimensional data, this work proposes new algorithms for reconstruction-based manifold learning within Reproducing-Kernel Hilbert Spaces (RKHS). Each observation is first reconstructed as a linear combination of the other samples in the RKHS, by optimizing a vector form of the Representer Theorem for their autorepresentation property.

Abstract: Urban perception describes how people subjectively evaluate urban environments, shaping how cities are experienced and understood. Research & Papers.title: Gradients with Respect to Semantics Preserving Embeddings Tell the Uncertainty of Large Language Models
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) is an important technique for ensuring the trustworthiness of LLMs, given their tendency to hallucinate. Research & Papers.title: CodeEvolve: LLM-Driven Evolutionary Optimization with Runtime-Enriched Target Selection for Multi-Language Code Enhancement
Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.title: Multi-Level Bidirectional Biomimetic Learning for EEG-Based Visual Decoding
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04680v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: EEG-based visual neural decoding aims to align neural responses with visual stimuli for tasks such as image retrieval. Research & Papers.title: Average Attention Transformers and Arithmetic Circuits
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: We analyse the computational power of transformer encoders as sequence-to-sequence functions on vectors. Research & Papers.title: FaithfulFaces: Pose-Faithful Facial Identity Preservation for Text-to-Video Generation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04702v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Identity-preserving text-to-video generation (IPT2V) empowers users to produce diverse and imaginative videos with consistent human facial identity. Research & Papers.title: Exact Dual Geometry of SOC-ICNN Value Functions

Research & Papers.title: From Beats to Breaches:How Offensive AI Infers Sensitive User Information from Playlists
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04724v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The pervasive integration of AI has enabled Offensive AI: the exploitation of AI for malicious ends across the cyber-kill chain. Research & Papers.title: AISSA: Implementation and Deployment of an AI-based Student Slides Analysis tool for Academic Presentations

Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Effective peer feedback is essential for developing critical reflection in higher education, yet its impact is often limited by the inconsistent quality of student-generated comments. Research & Papers.title: Hybrid Congestion Classification Framework Using Flow-Guided Attention and Empirical Mode Decomposition

Abstract: Accurate traffic congestion classification requires models that jointly capture roadway scene context and non-stationary traffic motion, yet most prior work treats these requirements in isolation. Research & Papers.title: Gyan: An Explainable Neuro-Symbolic Language Model
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Transformer based pre-trained large language models have become ubiquitous. Research & Papers.title: Cognitive Twins: Investigating Personalized Thinking Model Building and Its Performance Enhancement with Human-in-the-Loop

Abstract: This paper presents the Personalized Thinking Model (PTM), a hierarchical and interpretable learner representation designed for AI supported education. Research & Papers.title: Beyond Seeing Is Believing: On Crowdsourced Detection of Audiovisual Deepfakes
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04797v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Deepfakes are increasingly realistic and easy to produce, raising concerns about the reliability of human judgments in misinformation settings. Research & Papers.title: StoryAlign: Evaluating and Training Reward Models for Story Generation
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04831v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Story generation aims to automatically produce coherent, structured, and engaging narratives. Although large language models (LLMs) have significantly advanced text generation, stories generated by LLMs still diverge from human-authored works regarding complex narrative structure and human-aligned preferences. Research & Papers.title: Quantile-Free Uncertainty Quantification in Graph Neural Networks
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04847v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Uncertainty quantification (UQ) in graph neural networks (GNNs) is crucial in high-stakes domains but remains a significant challenge. In graph settings, message passing often relies on strong assumptions such as exchangeability, which are rarely satisfied in practice. Research & Papers.title: Assessing Cognitive Effort in L2 Idiomatic Processing: An Eye-Tracking Dataset
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: This paper presents the development and validation of an eye-tracking dataset designed to investigate how second-language (L2) learners process idiomatic expressions. While native speakers often rely on direct retrieval of figurative meanings, L2 speakers frequently adopt a literal-first approach, which incurs measurable cognitive costs. Research & Papers.title: Anticipating Innovation Using Large Language Models
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04875v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Forecasting innovation, intended as the emergence of new technological combinations, is a fundamental challenge for science and policy. Research & Papers.title: FairEnc: A Fair Vision-Language Model with Fair Vision and Text Encoders for Glaucoma Detection
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Automated glaucoma detection is critical for preventing irreversible vision loss and reducing the burden on healthcare systems. Research & Papers.title: On the (In-)Security of the Shuffling Defense in the Transformer Secure Inference
Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04901v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: For Transformer models, cryptographically secure inference ensures that the client learns only the final output, while the server learns nothing about the client's input. Research & Papers.title: Delta-Based Neural Architecture Search: LLM Fine-Tuning via Code Diffs
Research & Papers.summary:

arXiv:2605.04922v1 Announce Type: cross

Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04930v1 Announce Type: cross

Research & Papers.summary:
arXiv:2605.04939v1 Announce Type: cross

Abstract: Modern LLMs show mastery over an ever-growing range of skills, as well as the ability to compose them flexibly. Research & Papers.summary:

Research & Papers.title: Federated Learning for Early Prediction of EV Charging Demand

Research & Papers.title: Adaptive Learning Strategies for AoA-Based Outdoor Localization: A Comprehensive Framework
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Localization in 5G and 6G networks is essential for important use cases such as intelligent transportation, smart factories, and smart cities. Research & Papers.title: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks

arXiv:2605.05071v1 Announce Type: cross

Research & Papers.title: Driver-WM: A Driver-Centric Traffic-Conditioned Latent World Model for In-Cabin Dynamics Rollout
Research & Papers.summary:

Abstract: Safe L2/L3 driving automation requires anticipating human-in-the-loop reactions during shared-control transitions. Research & Papers.title: Continual Knowledge Updating in LLM Systems: Learning Through Multi-Timescale Memory Dynamics

Research & Papers.title: Adaptive Policy Selection and Fine-Tuning under Interaction Budgets for Offline-to-Online Reinforcement Learning

Research & Papers.title: Aes3D: Aesthetic Assessment in 3D Gaussian Splatting

Research & Papers.title: On the Hardness of Junking LLMs

Research & Papers.summary:

arXiv:2605.04552v1 Announce Type: new

Research & Papers.title: Adapting Large Language Models to a Low-Resource Agglutinative Language: A Comparative Study of LoRA and QLoRA for Bashkir

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) form implicit beliefs (posteriors over latent variables) from prompts, but we lack a mechanistic account of how these beliefs are encoded in representation space, how they update with new evidence, and how interventions reshape them. arXiv:2602.03396v3 Announce Type: replace 
Abstract: Proprietary large language models (LLMs) embody substantial economic value and are generally exposed only as black-box APIs, yet adversaries can still exploit their outputs to extract knowledge via distillation. Abstract: Sentences like "She will go to France or Spain, or perhaps to Germany or France." appear formally redundant, yet become acceptable in contexts such as "Mary will go to a philosophy program in France or Spain, or a mathematics program in Germany or France." Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated exceptional performance across diverse domains but are increasingly constrained by high inference latency.
